2603.17231.title: Neuron-Level Emotion Control in Speech-Generative Large Audio-Language Models
2603.17231.paper_summary: Large audio-language models (LALMs) can produce expressive speech, yet reliable emotion control remains elusive: conversions often miss the target affect and may degrade linguistic fidelity through refusals, hallucinations, or paraphrase. We present, to our knowledge, the first neuron-level study of emotion control in speech-generative LALMs and demonstrate that compact emotion-sensitive neurons (ESNs) are causally actionable, enabling training-free emotion steering at inference time. ESNs are identified via success-filtered activation aggregation enforcing both emotion realization and content preservation. Across three LALMs (Qwen2.5-Omni-7B, MiniCPM-o 4.5, Kimi-Audio), ESN interventions yield emotion-specific gains that generalize to unseen speakers and are supported by automatic and human evaluation. Controllability depends on selector design, mask sparsity, filtering, and intervention strength. Our results establish a mechanistic framework for training-free emotion control in speech generation.
2603.17231.paper_summary_zh: 大型音頻語言模型（LALMs）能夠產生富有表現力的語音，但可靠的情感控制仍然難以實現：轉換往往未能達到目標情感，並可能通過拒絕、幻想或改述來降低語言的真實性。我們提出了迄今為止首個針對語音生成LALMs的神經元級情感控制研究，並展示了緊湊的情感敏感神經元（ESNs）是可因果操作的，從而實現了在推理時無需訓練的情感引導。ESNs通過成功過濾的激活聚合來識別，強調情感實現和內容保留。在三個LALMs（Qwen2.5-Omni-7B、MiniCPM-o 4.5、Kimi-Audio）中，ESN干預產生了針對情感的特定增益，這些增益能夠推廣到未見過的說話者，並得到了自動和人工評估的支持。可控性取決於選擇器設計、掩碼稀疏性、過濾和干預強度。我們的結果建立了一個無需訓練的語音生成情感控制的機制框架。
2603.17231.author: Xiutian Zhao et.al.
2603.17231.authors: Xiutian Zhao, Ismail Rasim Ulgen, Philipp Koehn, Björn Schuller, Berrak Sisman
